   586→func (r *NodeRepository) CountStatesTimed(ctx context.Context, filters []*model.NodeFilter, column string) ([]*model.NodeStatesTimed, error) {
   587→	query, qerr := AccessCheck(ctx, sq.Select(column, "time_stamp", "count(*) as count").From("node")) // "cluster"?
   588→	if qerr != nil {
   589→		return nil, qerr
   590→	}
   591→
   592→	query = query.Join("node_state ON node_state.node_id = node.id")
   593→
   594→	for _, f := range filters {
   595→		// Required
   596→		if f.TimeStart != nil {
   597→			query = query.Where("time_stamp > ?", f.TimeStart)
   598→		}
   599→		// Optional
   600→		if f.Hostname != nil {
   601→			query = buildStringCondition("hostname", f.Hostname, query)
   602→		}
   603→		if f.Cluster != nil {
   604→			query = buildStringCondition("cluster", f.Cluster, query)
   605→		}
   606→		if f.SubCluster != nil {
   607→			query = buildStringCondition("subcluster", f.SubCluster, query)
   608→		}
   609→		if f.SchedulerState != nil {
   610→			query = query.Where("node_state = ?", f.SchedulerState)
   611→		}
   612→		if f.HealthState != nil {
   613→			query = query.Where("health_state = ?", f.HealthState)
   614→		}
   615→	}
   616→
   617→	// Add Group and Order
   618→	query = query.GroupBy(column + ", time_stamp").OrderBy("time_stamp ASC")
   619→
   620→	rows, err := query.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Query()
   621→	if err != nil {
   622→		queryString, queryVars, _ := query.ToSql()
   623→		cclog.Errorf("Error while running query '%s' %v: %v", queryString, queryVars, err)
   624→		return nil, err
   625→	}
   626→
   627→	rawData := make(map[string][][]int)
   628→	for rows.Next() {
   629→		var state string
   630→		var timestamp, count int
   631→
   632→		if err := rows.Scan(&state, &timestamp, &count); err != nil {
   633→			rows.Close()
   634→			cclog.Warnf("Error while scanning rows (CountStatesTimed) at time '%d'", timestamp)
   635→			return nil, err
   636→		}
   637→
   638→		if rawData[state] == nil {
   639→			rawData[state] = [][]int{make([]int, 0), make([]int, 0)}
   640→		}
   641→
   642→		rawData[state][0] = append(rawData[state][0], timestamp)
   643→		rawData[state][1] = append(rawData[state][1], count)
   644→	}
   645→
   646→	timedStates := make([]*model.NodeStatesTimed, 0)
   647→	for state, data := range rawData {
   648→		entry := model.NodeStatesTimed{State: state, Times: data[0], Counts: data[1]}
   649→		timedStates = append(timedStates, &entry)
   650→	}
   651→
   652→	return timedStates, nil
   653→}
   654→
   655→func (r *NodeRepository) GetNodesForList(
   656→	ctx context.Context,
   657→	cluster string,
   658→	subCluster string,
   659→	stateFilter string,
   660→	nodeFilter string,
   661→	page *model.PageRequest,
   662→) ([]string, map[string]string, int, bool, error) {
   663→	// Init Return Vars
   664→	nodes := make([]string, 0)
   665→	stateMap := make(map[string]string)
   666→	countNodes := 0
   667→	hasNextPage := false
   668→
   669→	// Build Filters
   670→	queryFilters := make([]*model.NodeFilter, 0)
   671→	if cluster != "" {
   672→		queryFilters = append(queryFilters, &model.NodeFilter{Cluster: &model.StringInput{Eq: &cluster}})
   673→	}
   674→	if subCluster != "" {
   675→		queryFilters = append(queryFilters, &model.NodeFilter{SubCluster: &model.StringInput{Eq: &subCluster}})
   676→	}
   677→	if nodeFilter != "" && stateFilter != "notindb" {
   678→		queryFilters = append(queryFilters, &model.NodeFilter{Hostname: &model.StringInput{Contains: &nodeFilter}})
   679→	}
   680→	if stateFilter != "all" && stateFilter != "notindb" {
   681→		queryState := schema.SchedulerState(stateFilter)
   682→		queryFilters = append(queryFilters, &model.NodeFilter{SchedulerState: &queryState})
   683→	}
   684→	// if healthFilter != "all" {
   685→	// 	filters = append(filters, &model.NodeFilter{HealthState: &healthFilter})
   686→	// }
   687→
   688→	// Special Case: Disable Paging for missing nodes filter, save IPP for later
   689→	var backupItems int
   690→	if stateFilter == "notindb" {
   691→		backupItems = page.ItemsPerPage
   692→		page.ItemsPerPage = -1
   693→	}
   694→
   695→	// Query Nodes From DB
   696→	rawNodes, serr := r.QueryNodes(ctx, queryFilters, page, nil) // Order not Used
   697→	if serr != nil {
   698→		cclog.Warn("error while loading node database data (Resolver.NodeMetricsList)")
   699→		return nil, nil, 0, false, serr
   700→	}
   701→
   702→	// Intermediate Node Result Info
   703→	for _, node := range rawNodes {
   704→		if node == nil {
   705→			continue
   706→		}
   707→		nodes = append(nodes, node.Hostname)
   708→		stateMap[node.Hostname] = string(node.NodeState)
   709→	}
   710→
   711→	// Special Case: Find Nodes not in DB node table but in metricStore only
   712→	if stateFilter == "notindb" {
   713→		// Reapply Original Paging
   714→		page.ItemsPerPage = backupItems
   715→		// Get Nodes From Topology
   716→		var topoNodes []string
   717→		if subCluster != "" {
   718→			scNodes := archive.NodeLists[cluster][subCluster]
   719→			topoNodes = scNodes.PrintList()
   720→		} else {
   721→			subClusterNodeLists := archive.NodeLists[cluster]
   722→			for _, nodeList := range subClusterNodeLists {
   723→				topoNodes = append(topoNodes, nodeList.PrintList()...)
   724→			}
   725→		}
   726→		// Compare to all nodes from cluster/subcluster in DB
   727→		var missingNodes []string
   728→		for _, scanNode := range topoNodes {
   729→			if !slices.Contains(nodes, scanNode) {
   730→				missingNodes = append(missingNodes, scanNode)
   731→			}
   732→		}
   733→		// Filter nodes by name
   734→		if nodeFilter != "" {
   735→			filteredNodesByName := []string{}
   736→			for _, missingNode := range missingNodes {
   737→				if strings.Contains(missingNode, nodeFilter) {
   738→					filteredNodesByName = append(filteredNodesByName, missingNode)
   739→				}
   740→			}
   741→			missingNodes = filteredNodesByName
   742→		}
   743→		// Sort Missing Nodes Alphanumerically
   744→		slices.Sort(missingNodes)
   745→		// Total Missing
   746→		countNodes = len(missingNodes)
   747→		// Apply paging
   748→		if countNodes > page.ItemsPerPage {
   749→			start := (page.Page - 1) * page.ItemsPerPage
   750→			end := start + page.ItemsPerPage
   751→			if end > countNodes {
   752→				end = countNodes
   753→				hasNextPage = false
   754→			} else {
   755→				hasNextPage = true
   756→			}
   757→			nodes = missingNodes[start:end]
   758→		} else {
   759→			nodes = missingNodes
   760→		}
   761→
   762→	} else {
   763→		// DB Nodes: Count and derive hasNextPage from count
   764→		var cerr error
   765→		countNodes, cerr = r.CountNodes(ctx, queryFilters)
   766→		if cerr != nil {
   767→			cclog.Warn("error while counting node database data (Resolver.NodeMetricsList)")
   768→			return nil, nil, 0, false, cerr
   769→		}
   770→		hasNextPage = page.Page*page.ItemsPerPage < countNodes
   771→	}
   772→
   773→	// Fallback for non-init'd node table in DB; Ignores stateFilter
   774→	if stateFilter == "all" && countNodes == 0 {
   775→		nodes, countNodes, hasNextPage = getNodesFromTopol(cluster, subCluster, nodeFilter, page)
   776→	}
   777→
   778→	return nodes, stateMap, countNodes, hasNextPage, nil
   779→}
   780→
   781→func AccessCheck(ctx context.Context, query sq.SelectBuilder) (sq.SelectBuilder, error) {
   782→	user := GetUserFromContext(ctx)
   783→	return AccessCheckWithUser(user, query)
   784→}
   785→
   786→func AccessCheckWithUser(user *schema.User, query sq.SelectBuilder) (sq.SelectBuilder, error) {
   787→	if user == nil {
   788→		var qnil sq.SelectBuilder
   789→		return qnil, fmt.Errorf("user context is nil")
   790→	}
   791→
   792→	switch {
   793→	// case len(user.Roles) == 1 && user.HasRole(schema.RoleApi): // API-User : Access NodeInfos
   794→	// 	return query, nil
   795→	case user.HasAnyRole([]schema.Role{schema.RoleAdmin, schema.RoleSupport}): // Admin & Support : Access NodeInfos
   796→		return query, nil
   797→	default: // No known Role: No Access, return error
   798→		var qnil sq.SelectBuilder
   799→		return qnil, fmt.Errorf("user has no or unknown roles")
   800→	}
   801→}
   802→
   803→func getNodesFromTopol(cluster string, subCluster string, nodeFilter string, page *model.PageRequest) ([]string, int, bool) {
   804→	// 0) Init additional vars
   805→	hasNextPage := false
   806→	totalNodes := 0
   807→
   808→	// 1) Get list of all nodes
   809→	var topolNodes []string
   810→	if subCluster != "" {
   811→		scNodes := archive.NodeLists[cluster][subCluster]
   812→		topolNodes = scNodes.PrintList()
   813→	} else {
   814→		subClusterNodeLists := archive.NodeLists[cluster]
   815→		for _, nodeList := range subClusterNodeLists {
   816→			topolNodes = append(topolNodes, nodeList.PrintList()...)
   817→		}
   818→	}
   819→
   820→	// 2) Filter nodes
   821→	if nodeFilter != "" {
   822→		filteredNodes := []string{}
   823→		for _, node := range topolNodes {
   824→			if strings.Contains(node, nodeFilter) {
   825→				filteredNodes = append(filteredNodes, node)
   826→			}
   827→		}
   828→		topolNodes = filteredNodes
   829→	}
   830→
   831→	// 2.1) Count total nodes && Sort nodes -> Sorting invalidated after ccms return ...
   832→	totalNodes = len(topolNodes)
   833→	sort.Strings(topolNodes)
   834→
   835→	// 3) Apply paging
   836→	if len(topolNodes) > page.ItemsPerPage {
   837→		start := (page.Page - 1) * page.ItemsPerPage
   838→		end := start + page.ItemsPerPage
   839→		if end >= len(topolNodes) {
   840→			end = len(topolNodes)
   841→			hasNextPage = false
   842→		} else {
   843→			hasNextPage = true
   844→		}
   845→		topolNodes = topolNodes[start:end]
   846→	}
   847→
   848→	return topolNodes, totalNodes, hasNextPage
   849→}

     5→
     6→// Package repository provides data access and persistence layer for ClusterCockpit.
     7→//
     8→// This file implements tag management functionality for job categorization and classification.
     9→// Tags support both manual assignment (via REST/GraphQL APIs) and automatic detection
    10→// (via tagger plugins). The implementation includes role-based access control through
    11→// tag scopes and maintains bidirectional consistency between the SQL database and
    12→// the file-based job archive.
    13→//
    14→// Database Schema:
    15→//
    16→//	CREATE TABLE tag (
    17→//	    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    18→//	    tag_type VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    19→//	    tag_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    20→//	    tag_scope VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL DEFAULT "global",
    21→//	    CONSTRAINT tag_unique UNIQUE (tag_type, tag_name, tag_scope)
    22→//	);
    23→//
    24→//	CREATE TABLE jobtag (
    25→//	    job_id INTEGER,
    26→//	    tag_id INTEGER,
    27→//	    PRIMARY KEY (job_id, tag_id),
    28→//	    FOREIGN KEY (job_id) REFERENCES job(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    29→//	    FOREIGN KEY (tag_id) REFERENCES tag(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
    30→//	);
    31→//
    32→// The jobtag junction table enables many-to-many relationships between jobs and tags.
    33→// CASCADE deletion ensures referential integrity when jobs or tags are removed.
    34→package repository
    35→
    36→import (
    37→	"errors"
    38→	"fmt"
    39→	"strings"
    40→
    41→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/pkg/archive"
    42→	cclog "github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/ccLogger"
    43→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/schema"
    44→	sq "github.com/Masterminds/squirrel"
    45→)
    46→
    47→// Tag Scope Rules:
    48→//
    49→// Tags in ClusterCockpit have three visibility scopes that control who can see and use them:
    50→//
    51→//  1. "global" - Visible to all users, can be used by anyone
    52→//     Example: System-generated tags like "energy-efficient", "failed", "short"
    53→//
    54→//  2. "private" - Only visible to the creating user
    55→//     Example: Personal notes like "needs-review", "interesting-case"
    56→//
    57→//  3. "admin" - Only visible to users with admin or support roles
    58→//     Example: Internal notes like "hardware-issue", "billing-problem"
    59→//
    60→// Authorization Rules:
    61→//   - Regular users can only create/see "global" and their own "private" tags
    62→//   - Admin/Support can create/see all scopes including "admin" tags
    63→//   - Users can only add tags to jobs they have permission to view
    64→//   - Tag scope is enforced at query time in GetTags() and CountTags()
    65→
    66→// AddTag adds the tag with id `tagId` to the job with the database id `jobId`.
    67→// Requires user authentication for security checks.
    68→//
    69→// The user must have permission to view the job. Tag visibility is determined by scope:
    70→//   - "global" tags: visible to all users
    71→//   - "private" tags: only visible to the tag creator
    72→//   - "admin" tags: only visible to admin/support users
    73→func (r *JobRepository) AddTag(user *schema.User, job int64, tag int64) ([]*schema.Tag, error) {
    74→	j, err := r.FindByIDWithUser(user, job)
    75→	if err != nil {
    76→		cclog.Warnf("Error finding job %d for user %s: %v", job, user.Username, err)
    77→		return nil, err
    78→	}
    79→
    80→	return r.addJobTag(job, tag, j, func() ([]*schema.Tag, error) {
    81→		return r.GetTags(user, &job)
    82→	})
    83→}
    84→
    85→// AddTagDirect adds a tag without user security checks.
    86→// Use only for internal/admin operations.
    87→func (r *JobRepository) AddTagDirect(job int64, tag int64) ([]*schema.Tag, error) {
    88→	j, err := r.FindByIDDirect(job)
    89→	if err != nil {
    90→		cclog.Warnf("Error finding job %d: %v", job, err)
    91→		return nil, err
    92→	}
    93→
    94→	return r.addJobTag(job, tag, j, func() ([]*schema.Tag, error) {
    95→		return r.GetTagsDirect(&job)
    96→	})
    97→}
    98→
    99→// RemoveTag removes the tag with the database id `tag` from the job with the database id `job`.
   100→// Requires user authentication for security checks. Used by GraphQL API.
   101→func (r *JobRepository) RemoveTag(user *schema.User, job, tag int64) ([]*schema.Tag, error) {
   102→	j, err := r.FindByIDWithUser(user, job)
   103→	if err != nil {
   104→		cclog.Warnf("Error while finding job %d for user %s during tag removal: %v", job, user.Username, err)
   105→		return nil, err
   106→	}
   107→
   108→	q := sq.Delete("jobtag").Where("jobtag.job_id = ?", job).Where("jobtag.tag_id = ?", tag)
   109→
   110→	if _, err := q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Exec(); err != nil {
   111→		s, _, _ := q.ToSql()
   112→		cclog.Errorf("Error removing tag with %s: %v", s, err)
   113→		return nil, err
   114→	}
   115→
   116→	tags, err := r.GetTags(user, &job)
   117→	if err != nil {
   118→		cclog.Warn("Error while getting tags for job")
   119→		return nil, err
   120→	}
   121→
   122→	archiveTags, err := r.getArchiveTags(&job)
   123→	if err != nil {
   124→		cclog.Warnf("Error while getting archive tags for job %d in RemoveTag: %v", job, err)
   125→		return nil, err
   126→	}
   127→
   128→	return tags, archive.UpdateTags(j, archiveTags)
   129→}
   130→
   131→// RemoveJobTagByRequest removes a tag from the job with the database id `job` by tag type, name, and scope.
   132→// Requires user authentication for security checks. Used by REST API.
   133→func (r *JobRepository) RemoveJobTagByRequest(user *schema.User, job int64, tagType string, tagName string, tagScope string) ([]*schema.Tag, error) {
   134→	// Get Tag ID to delete
   135→	tagID, exists := r.TagID(tagType, tagName, tagScope)
   136→	if !exists {
   137→		cclog.Warnf("Tag does not exist (name, type, scope): %s, %s, %s", tagName, tagType, tagScope)
   138→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("tag does not exist (name, type, scope): %s, %s, %s", tagName, tagType, tagScope)
   139→	}
   140→
   141→	// Get Job
   142→	j, err := r.FindByIDWithUser(user, job)
   143→	if err != nil {
   144→		cclog.Warnf("Error while finding job %d for user %s during tag removal by request: %v", job, user.Username, err)
   145→		return nil, err
   146→	}
   147→
   148→	// Handle Delete
   149→	q := sq.Delete("jobtag").Where("jobtag.job_id = ?", job).Where("jobtag.tag_id = ?", tagID)
   150→
   151→	if _, err := q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Exec(); err != nil {
   152→		s, _, _ := q.ToSql()
   153→		cclog.Errorf("Error removing tag from table 'jobTag' with %s: %v", s, err)
   154→		return nil, err
   155→	}
   156→
   157→	tags, err := r.GetTags(user, &job)
   158→	if err != nil {
   159→		cclog.Warnf("Error while getting tags for job %d in RemoveJobTagByRequest: %v", job, err)
   160→		return nil, err
   161→	}
   162→
   163→	archiveTags, err := r.getArchiveTags(&job)
   164→	if err != nil {
   165→		cclog.Warnf("Error while getting archive tags for job %d in RemoveJobTagByRequest: %v", job, err)
   166→		return nil, err
   167→	}
   168→
   169→	return tags, archive.UpdateTags(j, archiveTags)
   170→}
   171→
   172→// removeTagFromArchiveJobs updates the job archive for all affected jobs after a tag deletion.
   173→//
   174→// This function is called asynchronously (via goroutine) after removing a tag from the database
   175→// to synchronize the file-based job archive with the database state. Errors are logged but not
   176→// returned since this runs in the background.
   177→//
   178→// Parameters:
   179→//   - jobIds: Database IDs of all jobs that had the deleted tag
   180→//
   181→// Implementation note: Each job is processed individually to handle partial failures gracefully.
   182→// If one job fails to update, others will still be processed.
   183→func (r *JobRepository) removeTagFromArchiveJobs(jobIds []int64) {
   184→	for _, j := range jobIds {
   185→		tags, err := r.getArchiveTags(&j)
   186→		if err != nil {
   187→			cclog.Warnf("Error while getting tags for job %d", j)
   188→			continue
   189→		}
   190→
   191→		job, err := r.FindByIDDirect(j)
   192→		if err != nil {
   193→			cclog.Warnf("Error while getting job %d", j)
   194→			continue
   195→		}
   196→
   197→		archive.UpdateTags(job, tags)
   198→	}
   199→}
   200→
   201→// Removes a tag from db by tag info
   202→// Used by REST API. Does not update tagged jobs in Job archive.
   203→func (r *JobRepository) RemoveTagByRequest(tagType string, tagName string, tagScope string) error {
   204→	// Get Tag ID to delete
   205→	tagID, exists := r.TagID(tagType, tagName, tagScope)
   206→	if !exists {
   207→		cclog.Warnf("Tag does not exist (name, type, scope): %s, %s, %s", tagName, tagType, tagScope)
   208→		return fmt.Errorf("tag does not exist (name, type, scope): %s, %s, %s", tagName, tagType, tagScope)
   209→	}
   210→
   211→	return r.RemoveTagByID(tagID)
   212→}
   213→
   214→// Removes a tag from db by tag id
   215→// Used by GraphQL API.
   216→func (r *JobRepository) RemoveTagByID(tagID int64) error {
   217→	jobIds, err := r.FindJobIdsByTag(tagID)
   218→	if err != nil {
   219→		return err
   220→	}
   221→
   222→	// Handle Delete JobTagTable
   223→	qJobTag := sq.Delete("jobtag").Where("jobtag.tag_id = ?", tagID)
   224→
   225→	if _, err := qJobTag.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Exec(); err != nil {
   226→		s, _, _ := qJobTag.ToSql()
   227→		cclog.Errorf("Error removing tag from table 'jobTag' with %s: %v", s, err)
   228→		return err
   229→	}
   230→
   231→	// Handle Delete TagTable
   232→	qTag := sq.Delete("tag").Where("tag.id = ?", tagID)
   233→
   234→	if _, err := qTag.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Exec(); err != nil {
   235→		s, _, _ := qTag.ToSql()
   236→		cclog.Errorf("Error removing tag from table 'tag' with %s: %v", s, err)
   237→		return err
   238→	}
   239→
   240→	// asynchronously update archive jobs
   241→	go r.removeTagFromArchiveJobs(jobIds)
   242→
   243→	return nil
   244→}

   246→// CreateTag creates a new tag with the specified type, name, and scope.
   247→// Returns the database ID of the newly created tag.
   248→//
   249→// Scope defaults to "global" if empty string is provided.
   250→// Valid scopes: "global", "private", "admin"
   251→//
   252→// Example:
   253→//
   254→//	tagID, err := repo.CreateTag("performance", "high-memory", "global")
   255→func (r *JobRepository) CreateTag(tagType string, tagName string, tagScope string) (tagID int64, err error) {
   256→	// Default to "Global" scope if none defined
   257→	if tagScope == "" {
   258→		tagScope = "global"
   259→	}
   260→
   261→	q := sq.Insert("tag").Columns("tag_type", "tag_name", "tag_scope").Values(tagType, tagName, tagScope)
   262→
   263→	res, err := q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Exec()
   264→	if err != nil {
   265→		s, _, _ := q.ToSql()
   266→		cclog.Errorf("Error inserting tag with %s: %v", s, err)
   267→		return 0, err
   268→	}
   269→
   270→	return res.LastInsertId()
   271→}
   272→
   273→// CountTags returns all tags visible to the user and the count of jobs for each tag.
   274→// Applies scope-based filtering to respect tag visibility rules.
   275→//
   276→// Returns:
   277→//   - tags: slice of tags the user can see
   278→//   - counts: map of tag name to job count
   279→//   - err: any error encountered
   280→func (r *JobRepository) CountTags(user *schema.User) (tags []schema.Tag, counts map[string]int, err error) {
   281→	tags = make([]schema.Tag, 0, 100)
   282→	xrows, err := r.DB.Queryx("SELECT id, tag_type, tag_name, tag_scope FROM tag")
   283→	if err != nil {
   284→		return nil, nil, err
   285→	}
   286→
   287→	for xrows.Next() {
   288→		var t schema.Tag
   289→		if err = xrows.StructScan(&t); err != nil {
   290→			return nil, nil, err
   291→		}
   292→
   293→		// Handle Scope Filtering: Tag Scope is Global, Private (== Username) or User is auth'd to view Admin Tags
   294→		readable, err := r.checkScopeAuth(user, "read", t.Scope)
   295→		if err != nil {
   296→			return nil, nil, err
   297→		}
   298→		if readable {
   299→			tags = append(tags, t)
   300→		}
   301→	}
   302→
   303→	// Query and Count Jobs with attached Tags
   304→	q := sq.Select("t.tag_type, t.tag_name, t.id, count(jt.tag_id)").
   305→		From("tag t").
   306→		LeftJoin("jobtag jt ON t.id = jt.tag_id").
   307→		GroupBy("t.tag_type, t.tag_name")
   308→
   309→	// Build scope list for filtering
   310→	var scopeBuilder strings.Builder
   311→	scopeBuilder.WriteString(`"global"`)
   312→	if user != nil {
   313→		scopeBuilder.WriteString(`,"`)
   314→		scopeBuilder.WriteString(user.Username)
   315→		scopeBuilder.WriteString(`"`)
   316→		if user.HasAnyRole([]schema.Role{schema.RoleAdmin, schema.RoleSupport}) {
   317→			scopeBuilder.WriteString(`,"admin"`)
   318→		}
   319→	}
   320→	q = q.Where("t.tag_scope IN (" + scopeBuilder.String() + ")")
   321→
   322→	// Handle Job Ownership
   323→	if user != nil && user.HasAnyRole([]schema.Role{schema.RoleAdmin, schema.RoleSupport}) { // ADMIN || SUPPORT: Count all jobs
   324→		// cclog.Debug("CountTags: User Admin or Support -> Count all Jobs for Tags")
   325→		// Unchanged: Needs to be own case still, due to UserRole/NoRole compatibility handling in else case
   326→	} else if user != nil && user.HasRole(schema.RoleManager) { // MANAGER: Count own jobs plus project's jobs
   327→		// Build ("project1", "project2", ...) list of variable length directly in SQL string
   328→		q = q.Where("jt.job_id IN (SELECT id FROM job WHERE job.hpc_user = ? OR job.project IN (\""+strings.Join(user.Projects, "\",\"")+"\"))", user.Username)
   329→	} else if user != nil { // USER OR NO ROLE (Compatibility): Only count own jobs
   330→		q = q.Where("jt.job_id IN (SELECT id FROM job WHERE job.hpc_user = ?)", user.Username)
   331→	}
   332→
   333→	rows, err := q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Query()
   334→	if err != nil {
   335→		return nil, nil, err
   336→	}
   337→
   338→	counts = make(map[string]int)
   339→	for rows.Next() {
   340→		var tagType string
   341→		var tagName string
   342→		var tagID int
   343→		var count int
   344→		if err = rows.Scan(&tagType, &tagName, &tagID, &count); err != nil {
   345→			return nil, nil, err
   346→		}
   347→		// Use tagId as second Map-Key component to differentiate tags with identical names
   348→		counts[fmt.Sprint(tagType, tagName, tagID)] = count
   349→	}
   350→	err = rows.Err()
   351→
   352→	return tags, counts, err
   353→}
   354→
   355→var (
   356→	// ErrTagNotFound is returned when a tag ID or tag identifier (type, name, scope) does not exist in the database.
   357→	ErrTagNotFound = errors.New("the tag does not exist")
   358→
   359→	// ErrJobNotOwned is returned when a user attempts to tag a job they do not have permission to access.
   360→	ErrJobNotOwned = errors.New("user is not owner of job")
   361→
   362→	// ErrTagNoAccess is returned when a user attempts to use a tag they cannot access due to scope restrictions.
   363→	ErrTagNoAccess = errors.New("user not permitted to use that tag")
   364→
   365→	// ErrTagPrivateScope is returned when a user attempts to access another user's private tag.
   366→	ErrTagPrivateScope = errors.New("tag is private to another user")
   367→
   368→	// ErrTagAdminScope is returned when a non-admin user attempts to use an admin-scoped tag.
   369→	ErrTagAdminScope = errors.New("tag requires admin privileges")
   370→
   371→	// ErrTagsIncompatScopes is returned when attempting to combine admin and non-admin scoped tags in a single operation.
   372→	ErrTagsIncompatScopes = errors.New("combining admin and non-admin scoped tags not allowed")
   373→)
   374→
   375→// addJobTag is a helper function that inserts a job-tag association and updates the archive.
   376→//
   377→// This function performs three operations atomically:
   378→//  1. Inserts the job-tag association into the jobtag junction table
   379→//  2. Retrieves the updated tag list for the job (using the provided getTags callback)
   380→//  3. Updates the job archive with the new tags to maintain database-archive consistency
   381→//
   382→// Parameters:
   383→//   - jobId: Database ID of the job
   384→//   - tagId: Database ID of the tag to associate
   385→//   - job: Full job object needed for archive update
   386→//   - getTags: Callback function to retrieve updated tags (allows different security contexts)
   387→//
   388→// Returns the complete updated tag list for the job or an error.
   389→//
   390→// Note: This function does NOT validate tag scope permissions - callers must perform
   391→// authorization checks before invoking this helper.
   392→func (r *JobRepository) addJobTag(jobID int64, tagID int64, job *schema.Job, getTags func() ([]*schema.Tag, error)) ([]*schema.Tag, error) {
   393→	q := sq.Insert("jobtag").Columns("job_id", "tag_id").Values(jobID, tagID)
   394→
   395→	if _, err := q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Exec(); err != nil {
   396→		s, _, _ := q.ToSql()
   397→		cclog.Errorf("Error adding tag with %s: %v", s, err)
   398→		return nil, err
   399→	}
   400→
   401→	tags, err := getTags()
   402→	if err != nil {
   403→		cclog.Warnf("Error getting tags for job %d: %v", jobID, err)
   404→		return nil, err
   405→	}
   406→
   407→	archiveTags, err := r.getArchiveTags(&jobID)
   408→	if err != nil {
   409→		cclog.Warnf("Error getting archive tags for job %d: %v", jobID, err)
   410→		return nil, err
   411→	}
   412→
   413→	return tags, archive.UpdateTags(job, archiveTags)
   414→}
   415→
   416→// AddTagOrCreate adds the tag with the specified type and name to the job with the database id `jobId`.
   417→// If such a tag does not yet exist, it is created.
   418→func (r *JobRepository) AddTagOrCreate(user *schema.User, jobID int64, tagType string, tagName string, tagScope string) (tagID int64, err error) {
   419→	// Default to "Global" scope if none defined
   420→	if tagScope == "" {
   421→		tagScope = "global"
   422→	}
   423→
   424→	writable, err := r.checkScopeAuth(user, "write", tagScope)
   425→	if err != nil {
   426→		return 0, err
   427→	}
   428→	if !writable {
   429→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("cannot write tag scope with current authorization")
   430→	}
   431→
   432→	tagID, exists := r.TagID(tagType, tagName, tagScope)
   433→	if !exists {
   434→		tagID, err = r.CreateTag(tagType, tagName, tagScope)
   435→		if err != nil {
   436→			return 0, err
   437→		}
   438→	}
   439→
   440→	if _, err := r.AddTag(user, jobID, tagID); err != nil {
   441→		return 0, err
   442→	}
   443→
   444→	return tagID, nil
   445→}
   446→
   447→func (r *JobRepository) AddTagOrCreateDirect(jobID int64, tagType string, tagName string) (tagID int64, err error) {
   448→	tagScope := "global"
   449→
   450→	tagID, exists := r.TagID(tagType, tagName, tagScope)
   451→	if !exists {
   452→		tagID, err = r.CreateTag(tagType, tagName, tagScope)
   453→		if err != nil {
   454→			return 0, err
   455→		}
   456→	}
   457→
   458→	cclog.Infof("Adding tag %s:%s:%s (direct)", tagType, tagName, tagScope)
   459→
   460→	if _, err := r.AddTagDirect(jobID, tagID); err != nil {
   461→		return 0, err
   462→	}
   463→
   464→	return tagID, nil
   465→}

   467→func (r *JobRepository) HasTag(jobID int64, tagType string, tagName string) bool {
   468→	var id int64
   469→	q := sq.Select("id").From("tag").Join("jobtag ON jobtag.tag_id = tag.id").
   470→		Where("jobtag.job_id = ?", jobID).Where("tag.tag_type = ?", tagType).
   471→		Where("tag.tag_name = ?", tagName)
   472→	err := q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).QueryRow().Scan(&id)
   473→	if err != nil {
   474→		return false
   475→	} else {
   476→		return true
   477→	}
   478→}

   481→func (r *JobRepository) TagID(tagType string, tagName string, tagScope string) (tagID int64, exists bool) {
   482→	exists = true
   483→	if err := sq.Select("id").From("tag").
   484→		Where("tag.tag_type = ?", tagType).Where("tag.tag_name = ?", tagName).Where("tag.tag_scope = ?", tagScope).
   485→		RunWith(r.stmtCache).QueryRow().Scan(&tagID); err != nil {
   486→		exists = false
   487→	}
   488→	return

   491→// TagInfo returns the database infos of the tag with the specified id.
   492→func (r *JobRepository) TagInfo(tagID int64) (tagType string, tagName string, tagScope string, exists bool) {
   493→	exists = true
   494→	if err := sq.Select("tag.tag_type", "tag.tag_name", "tag.tag_scope").From("tag").Where("tag.id = ?", tagID).
   495→		RunWith(r.stmtCache).QueryRow().Scan(&tagType, &tagName, &tagScope); err != nil {
   496→		exists = false
   497→	}
   498→	return

   501→// GetTags returns a list of all scoped tags if job is nil or of the tags that the job with that database ID has.
   502→func (r *JobRepository) GetTags(user *schema.User, job *int64) ([]*schema.Tag, error) {
   503→	q := sq.Select("id", "tag_type", "tag_name", "tag_scope").From("tag")
   504→	if job != nil {
   505→		q = q.Join("jobtag ON jobtag.tag_id = tag.id").Where("jobtag.job_id = ?", *job)
   506→	}
   507→
   508→	rows, err := q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Query()

   562→//
   563→// This internal function is used exclusively for job archive synchronization where we need
   564→// to store all tags regardless of the current user's permissions. Unlike GetTags() which

   573→func (r *JobRepository) getArchiveTags(job *int64) ([]*schema.Tag, error) {

   621→// checkScopeAuth validates whether a user is authorized to perform an operation on a tag with the given scope.
   622→//
   623→// This function implements the tag scope authorization matrix:
   624→//
   625→//	Scope        | Read Access                      | Write Access
   626→//	-------------|----------------------------------|----------------------------------
   627→//	"global"     | All users                        | Admin, Support, API-only
   628→//	"admin"      | Admin, Support                   | Admin, API-only
   629→//	<username>   | Owner only                       | Owner only (private tags)
   630→//
   631→// Parameters:
   632→//   - user: User attempting the operation (must not be nil)
   633→//   - operation: Either "read" or "write"
   634→//   - scope: Tag scope value ("global", "admin", or username for private tags)
   635→//
   636→// Returns:
   637→//   - pass: true if authorized, false if denied
   638→//   - err: error only if operation is invalid or user is nil
   639→//
   640→// Special cases:
   641→//   - API-only users (single role: RoleApi) can write to admin and global scopes for automation
   642→//   - Private tags use the username as scope, granting exclusive access to that user
   643→func (r *JobRepository) checkScopeAuth(user *schema.User, operation string, scope string) (pass bool, err error) {
   644→	if user != nil {
   645→		switch {
   646→		case operation == "write" && scope == "admin":
   647→			if user.HasRole(schema.RoleAdmin) || (len(user.Roles) == 1 && user.HasRole(schema.RoleAPI)) {
   648→				return true, nil
   649→			}
   650→			return false, nil
   651→		case operation == "write" && scope == "global":
   652→			if user.HasAnyRole([]schema.Role{schema.RoleAdmin, schema.RoleSupport}) || (len(user.Roles) == 1 && user.HasRole(schema.RoleAPI)) {
   653→				return true, nil
   654→			}
   655→			return false, nil
   656→		case operation == "write" && scope == user.Username:
   657→			return true, nil
   658→		case operation == "read" && scope == "admin":
   659→			return user.HasAnyRole([]schema.Role{schema.RoleAdmin, schema.RoleSupport}), nil
   660→		case operation == "read" && scope == "global":
   661→			return true, nil
   662→		case operation == "read" && scope == user.Username:
   663→			return true, nil
   664→		default:
   665→			if operation == "read" || operation == "write" {
   666→				// No acceptable scope: deny tag
   667→				return false, nil
   668→			} else {
   669→				return false, fmt.Errorf("error while checking tag operation auth: unknown operation (%s)", operation)
   670→			}
   671→		}
   672→	} else {
   673→		return false, fmt.Errorf("error while checking tag operation auth: no user in context")
   674→	}
   675→}

1→// Copyright (C) NHR@FAU, University Erlangen-Nuremberg.

     5→
     6→// This file contains job statistics and histogram generation functionality for the JobRepository.
     7→//
     8→// # Job Statistics
     9→//
    10→// The statistics methods provide aggregated metrics about jobs including total jobs, users,
    11→// walltime, and resource usage (nodes, cores, accelerators). Statistics can be computed:
    12→//   - Overall (JobsStats): Single aggregate across all matching jobs
    13→//   - Grouped (JobsStatsGrouped): Aggregated by user, project, cluster, or subcluster
    14→//   - Counts (JobCountGrouped, AddJobCount): Simple job counts with optional filtering
    15→//
    16→// All statistics methods support filtering via JobFilter and respect security contexts.
    17→//
    18→// # Histograms
    19→//
    20→// Histogram methods generate distribution data for visualization:
    21→//   - Duration, nodes, cores, accelerators (AddHistograms)
    22→//   - Job metrics like CPU load, memory usage (AddMetricHistograms)
    23→//
    24→// Histograms use intelligent binning:
    25→//   - Duration: Variable bin sizes (1m, 10m, 1h, 6h, 12h, 24h) with zero-padding
    26→//   - Resources: Natural value-based bins
    27→//   - Metrics: Normalized to peak values with configurable bin counts
    28→//
    29→// # Running vs. Completed Jobs
    30→//
    31→// Statistics handle running jobs specially:
    32→//   - Duration calculated as (now - start_time) for running jobs
    33→//   - Metric histograms for running jobs load data from metric backend instead of footprint
    34→//   - Job state filtering distinguishes running/completed jobs
    35→//
    36→// # Performance Considerations
    37→//
    38→// - All queries use prepared statements via stmtCache
    39→// - Complex aggregations use SQL for efficiency
    40→// - Histogram pre-initialization ensures consistent bin ranges
    41→// - Metric histogram queries limited to 5000 jobs for running job analysis
    42→
    43→package repository
    44→
    45→import (
    46→	"context"
    47→	"database/sql"
    48→	"fmt"
    49→	"time"
    50→
    51→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/config"
    52→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/graph/model"
    53→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/metricdispatch"
    54→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/pkg/archive"
    55→	cclog "github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/ccLogger"
    56→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/schema"
    57→	sq "github.com/Masterminds/squirrel"
    58→)
    59→
    60→// groupBy2column maps GraphQL Aggregate enum values to their corresponding database column names.
    61→// Used by JobsStatsGrouped and JobCountGrouped to translate user-facing grouping dimensions
    62→// into SQL GROUP BY clauses. GraphQL validation ensures only valid enum values are accepted.
    63→var groupBy2column = map[model.Aggregate]string{
    64→	model.AggregateUser:       "job.hpc_user",
    65→	model.AggregateProject:    "job.project",
    66→	model.AggregateCluster:    "job.cluster",
    67→	model.AggregateSubcluster: "job.subcluster",
    68→}
    69→
    70→// sortBy2column maps GraphQL SortByAggregate enum values to their corresponding computed column names.
    71→// Used by JobsStatsGrouped to translate sort preferences into SQL ORDER BY clauses.
    72→// Column names match the AS aliases used in buildStatsQuery.
    73→var sortBy2column = map[model.SortByAggregate]string{
    74→	model.SortByAggregateTotaljobs:      "totalJobs",
    75→	model.SortByAggregateTotalusers:     "totalUsers",
    76→	model.SortByAggregateTotalwalltime:  "totalWalltime",
    77→	model.SortByAggregateTotalnodes:     "totalNodes",
    78→	model.SortByAggregateTotalnodehours: "totalNodeHours",
    79→	model.SortByAggregateTotalcores:     "totalCores",
    80→	model.SortByAggregateTotalcorehours: "totalCoreHours",
    81→	model.SortByAggregateTotalaccs:      "totalAccs",
    82→	model.SortByAggregateTotalacchours:  "totalAccHours",
    83→}
    84→
    85→// buildCountQuery constructs a SQL query to count jobs with optional grouping and filtering.
    86→//
    87→// Parameters:
    88→//   - filter: Job filters to apply (cluster, user, time range, etc.)
    89→//   - kind: Special filter - "running" for running jobs only, "short" for jobs under threshold
    90→//   - col: Column name to GROUP BY; empty string for total count without grouping
    91→//
    92→// Returns a SelectBuilder that produces either:
    93→//   - Single count: COUNT(job.id) when col is empty
    94→//   - Grouped counts: col, COUNT(job.id) when col is specified
    95→//
    96→// The kind parameter enables counting specific job categories:
    97→//   - "running": Only jobs with job_state = 'running'
    98→//   - "short": Only jobs with duration < ShortRunningJobsDuration config value
    99→//   - empty: All jobs matching filters
   100→func (r *JobRepository) buildCountQuery(
   101→	filter []*model.JobFilter,
   102→	kind string,
   103→	col string,
   104→) sq.SelectBuilder {
   105→	var query sq.SelectBuilder
   106→
   107→	if col != "" {
   108→		query = sq.Select(col, "COUNT(job.id)").From("job").GroupBy(col)
   109→	} else {
   110→		query = sq.Select("COUNT(job.id)").From("job")
   111→	}
   112→
   113→	switch kind {
   114→	case "running":
   115→		query = query.Where("job.job_state = ?", "running")
   116→	case "short":
   117→		query = query.Where("job.duration < ?", config.Keys.ShortRunningJobsDuration)
   118→	}
   119→
   120→	for _, f := range filter {
   121→		query = BuildWhereClause(f, query)
   122→	}
   123→
   124→	return query
   125→}
   126→
   127→// buildStatsQuery constructs a SQL query to compute comprehensive job statistics with optional grouping.
   128→//
   129→// Parameters:
   130→//   - filter: Job filters to apply (cluster, user, time range, etc.)
   131→//   - col: Column name to GROUP BY; empty string for overall statistics without grouping
   132→//
   133→// Returns a SelectBuilder that produces comprehensive statistics:
   134→//   - totalJobs: Count of jobs
   135→//   - totalUsers: Count of distinct users (always 0 when grouping by user)
   136→//   - totalWalltime: Sum of job durations in hours
   137→//   - totalNodes: Sum of nodes used across all jobs
   138→//   - totalNodeHours: Sum of (duration × num_nodes) in hours
   139→//   - totalCores: Sum of hardware threads used across all jobs
   140→//   - totalCoreHours: Sum of (duration × num_hwthreads) in hours
   141→//   - totalAccs: Sum of accelerators used across all jobs
   142→//   - totalAccHours: Sum of (duration × num_acc) in hours
   143→//
   144→// Special handling:
   145→//   - Running jobs: Duration calculated as (now - start_time) instead of stored duration
   146→//   - Grouped queries: Also select grouping column and user's display name from hpc_user table
   147→//   - All time values converted from seconds to hours (÷ 3600) and rounded
   148→func (r *JobRepository) buildStatsQuery(
   149→	filter []*model.JobFilter,
   150→	col string,
   151→) sq.SelectBuilder {
   152→	var query sq.SelectBuilder
   153→
   154→	if col != "" {
   155→		query = sq.Select(
   156→			col,
   157→			"name",
   158→			"COUNT(job.id) as totalJobs",
   159→			"COUNT(DISTINCT job.hpc_user) AS totalUsers",
   160→			fmt.Sprintf(`CAST(ROUND(SUM((CASE WHEN job.job_state = "running" THEN %d - job.start_time ELSE job.duration END)) / 3600) as int) as totalWalltime`, time.Now().Unix()),
   161→			`CAST(SUM(job.num_nodes) as int) as totalNodes`,
   162→			fmt.Sprintf(`CAST(ROUND(SUM((CASE WHEN job.job_state = "running" THEN %d - job.start_time ELSE job.duration END) * job.num_nodes) / 3600) as int) as totalNodeHours`, time.Now().Unix()),
   163→			`CAST(SUM(job.num_hwthreads) as int) as totalCores`,
   164→			fmt.Sprintf(`CAST(ROUND(SUM((CASE WHEN job.job_state = "running" THEN %d - job.start_time ELSE job.duration END) * job.num_hwthreads) / 3600) as int) as totalCoreHours`, time.Now().Unix()),
   165→			`CAST(SUM(job.num_acc) as int) as totalAccs`,
   166→			fmt.Sprintf(`CAST(ROUND(SUM((CASE WHEN job.job_state = "running" THEN %d - job.start_time ELSE job.duration END) * job.num_acc) / 3600) as int) as totalAccHours`, time.Now().Unix()),
   167→		).From("job").LeftJoin("hpc_user ON hpc_user.username = job.hpc_user").GroupBy(col)
   168→	} else {
   169→		query = sq.Select(
   170→			"COUNT(job.id) as totalJobs",
   171→			"COUNT(DISTINCT job.hpc_user) AS totalUsers",
   172→			fmt.Sprintf(`CAST(ROUND(SUM((CASE WHEN job.job_state = "running" THEN %d - job.start_time ELSE job.duration END)) / 3600) as int)`, time.Now().Unix()),
   173→			`CAST(SUM(job.num_nodes) as int)`,
   174→			fmt.Sprintf(`CAST(ROUND(SUM((CASE WHEN job.job_state = "running" THEN %d - job.start_time ELSE job.duration END) * job.num_nodes) / 3600) as int)`, time.Now().Unix()),
   175→			`CAST(SUM(job.num_hwthreads) as int)`,
   176→			fmt.Sprintf(`CAST(ROUND(SUM((CASE WHEN job.job_state = "running" THEN %d - job.start_time ELSE job.duration END) * job.num_hwthreads) / 3600) as int)`, time.Now().Unix()),
   177→			`CAST(SUM(job.num_acc) as int)`,
   178→			fmt.Sprintf(`CAST(ROUND(SUM((CASE WHEN job.job_state = "running" THEN %d - job.start_time ELSE job.duration END) * job.num_acc) / 3600) as int)`, time.Now().Unix()),
   179→		).From("job")
   180→	}
   181→
   182→	for _, f := range filter {
   183→		query = BuildWhereClause(f, query)
   184→	}
   185→
   186→	return query
   187→}
   188→
   189→// JobsStatsGrouped computes comprehensive job statistics grouped by a dimension (user, project, cluster, or subcluster).
   190→//
   191→// This is the primary method for generating aggregated statistics views in the UI, providing
   192→// metrics like total jobs, walltime, and resource usage broken down by the specified grouping.
   193→//
   194→// Parameters:
   195→//   - ctx: Context for security checks and cancellation
   196→//   - filter: Filters to apply (time range, cluster, job state, etc.)
   197→//   - page: Optional pagination (ItemsPerPage: -1 disables pagination)
   198→//   - sortBy: Optional sort column (totalJobs, totalWalltime, totalCoreHours, etc.)
   199→//   - groupBy: Required grouping dimension (User, Project, Cluster, or SubCluster)
   200→//
   201→// Returns a slice of JobsStatistics, one per group, with:
   202→//   - ID: The group identifier (username, project name, cluster name, etc.)
   203→//   - Name: Display name (for users, from hpc_user.name; empty for other groups)
   204→//   - Statistics: totalJobs, totalUsers, totalWalltime, resource usage metrics
   205→//
   206→// Security: Respects user roles via SecurityCheck - users see only their own data unless admin/support.
   207→// Performance: Results are sorted in SQL and pagination applied before scanning rows.
   208→func (r *JobRepository) JobsStatsGrouped(
   209→	ctx context.Context,
   210→	filter []*model.JobFilter,
   211→	page *model.PageRequest,
   212→	sortBy *model.SortByAggregate,
   213→	groupBy *model.Aggregate,
   214→) ([]*model.JobsStatistics, error) {
   215→	start := time.Now()
   216→	col := groupBy2column[*groupBy]
   217→	query := r.buildStatsQuery(filter, col)
   218→
   219→	query, err := SecurityCheck(ctx, query)
   220→	if err != nil {
   221→		return nil, err
   222→	}
   223→
   224→	if sortBy != nil {
   225→		sortBy := sortBy2column[*sortBy]
   226→		query = query.OrderBy(fmt.Sprintf("%s DESC", sortBy))
   227→	}
   228→	if page != nil && page.ItemsPerPage != -1 {
   229→		limit := uint64(page.ItemsPerPage)
   230→		query = query.Offset((uint64(page.Page) - 1) * limit).Limit(limit)
   231→	}
   232→
   233→	rows, err := query.RunWith(r.DB).Query()
   234→	if err != nil {
   235→		cclog.Warn("Error while querying DB for job statistics")
   236→		return nil, err
   237→	}
   238→
   239→	stats := make([]*model.JobsStatistics, 0, 100)
   240→
   241→	for rows.Next() {
   242→		var id sql.NullString
   243→		var name sql.NullString
   244→		var jobs, users, walltime, nodes, nodeHours, cores, coreHours, accs, accHours sql.NullInt64
   245→		if err := rows.Scan(&id, &name, &jobs, &users, &walltime, &nodes, &nodeHours, &cores, &coreHours, &accs, &accHours); err != nil {
   246→			cclog.Warnf("Error while scanning rows: %s", err.Error())
   247→			return nil, err
   248→		}
   249→
   250→		if id.Valid {
   251→			var totalJobs, totalUsers, totalWalltime, totalNodes, totalNodeHours, totalCores, totalCoreHours, totalAccs, totalAccHours int
   252→			var personName string
   253→
   254→			if name.Valid {
   255→				personName = name.String
   256→			}
   257→
   258→			if jobs.Valid {
   259→				totalJobs = int(jobs.Int64)
   260→			}
   261→
   262→			if users.Valid {
   263→				totalUsers = int(users.Int64)
   264→			}
   265→
   266→			if walltime.Valid {
   267→				totalWalltime = int(walltime.Int64)
   268→			}
   269→
   270→			if nodes.Valid {
   271→				totalNodes = int(nodes.Int64)
   272→			}
   273→			if cores.Valid {
   274→				totalCores = int(cores.Int64)
   275→			}
   276→			if accs.Valid {
   277→				totalAccs = int(accs.Int64)
   278→			}
   279→
   280→			if nodeHours.Valid {
   281→				totalNodeHours = int(nodeHours.Int64)
   282→			}
   283→			if coreHours.Valid {
   284→				totalCoreHours = int(coreHours.Int64)
   285→			}
   286→			if accHours.Valid {
   287→				totalAccHours = int(accHours.Int64)
   288→			}
   289→
   290→			if col == "job.hpc_user" {
   291→				// name := r.getUserName(ctx, id.String)
   292→				stats = append(stats,
   293→					&model.JobsStatistics{
   294→						ID:             id.String,
   295→						Name:           personName,
   296→						TotalJobs:      totalJobs,
   297→						TotalWalltime:  totalWalltime,
   298→						TotalNodes:     totalNodes,
   299→						TotalNodeHours: totalNodeHours,
   300→						TotalCores:     totalCores,
   301→						TotalCoreHours: totalCoreHours,
   302→						TotalAccs:      totalAccs,
   303→						TotalAccHours:  totalAccHours,
   304→					})
   305→			} else {
   306→				stats = append(stats,
   307→					&model.JobsStatistics{
   308→						ID:             id.String,
   309→						TotalJobs:      totalJobs,
   310→						TotalUsers:     totalUsers,
   311→						TotalWalltime:  totalWalltime,
   312→						TotalNodes:     totalNodes,
   313→						TotalNodeHours: totalNodeHours,
   314→						TotalCores:     totalCores,
   315→						TotalCoreHours: totalCoreHours,
   316→						TotalAccs:      totalAccs,
   317→						TotalAccHours:  totalAccHours,
   318→					})
   319→			}
   320→		}
   321→	}
   322→
   323→	cclog.Debugf("Timer JobsStatsGrouped %s", time.Since(start))
   324→	return stats, nil
   325→}
   326→
   327→// JobsStats computes overall job statistics across all matching jobs without grouping.
   328→//
   329→// This method provides a single aggregate view of job metrics, useful for dashboard
   330→// summaries and overall system utilization reports.
   331→//
   332→// Parameters:
   333→//   - ctx: Context for security checks and cancellation
   334→//   - filter: Filters to apply (time range, cluster, job state, etc.)
   335→//
   336→// Returns a single-element slice containing aggregate statistics:
   337→//   - totalJobs, totalUsers, totalWalltime
   338→//   - totalNodeHours, totalCoreHours, totalAccHours
   339→//
   340→// Unlike JobsStatsGrouped, this returns overall totals without breaking down by dimension.
   341→// Security checks are applied via SecurityCheck to respect user access levels.
   342→func (r *JobRepository) JobsStats(
   343→	ctx context.Context,
   344→	filter []*model.JobFilter,
   345→) ([]*model.JobsStatistics, error) {
   346→	start := time.Now()
   347→	query := r.buildStatsQuery(filter, "")
   348→	query, err := SecurityCheck(ctx, query)
   349→	if err != nil {
   350→		return nil, err
   351→	}
   352→
   353→	row := query.RunWith(r.DB).QueryRow()
   354→	stats := make([]*model.JobsStatistics, 0, 1)
   355→
   356→	var jobs, users, walltime, nodes, nodeHours, cores, coreHours, accs, accHours sql.NullInt64
   357→	if err := row.Scan(&jobs, &users, &walltime, &nodes, &nodeHours, &cores, &coreHours, &accs, &accHours); err != nil {
   358→		cclog.Warn("Error while scanning rows")
   359→		return nil, err
   360→	}
   361→
   362→	if jobs.Valid {
   363→		var totalNodeHours, totalCoreHours, totalAccHours int
   364→
   365→		if nodeHours.Valid {
   366→			totalNodeHours = int(nodeHours.Int64)
   367→		}
   368→		if coreHours.Valid {
   369→			totalCoreHours = int(coreHours.Int64)
   370→		}
   371→		if accHours.Valid {
   372→			totalAccHours = int(accHours.Int64)
   373→		}
   374→		stats = append(stats,
   375→			&model.JobsStatistics{
   376→				TotalJobs:      int(jobs.Int64),
   377→				TotalUsers:     int(users.Int64),
   378→				TotalWalltime:  int(walltime.Int64),
   379→				TotalNodeHours: totalNodeHours,
   380→				TotalCoreHours: totalCoreHours,
   381→				TotalAccHours:  totalAccHours,
   382→			})
   383→	}
   384→
   385→	cclog.Debugf("Timer JobStats %s", time.Since(start))
   386→	return stats, nil
   387→}
   388→
   389→// LoadJobStat retrieves a specific statistic for a metric from a job's statistics.
   390→// Returns 0.0 if the metric is not found or statType is invalid.
   391→//
   392→// Parameters:
   393→//   - job: Job struct with populated Statistics field
   394→//   - metric: Name of the metric to query (e.g., "cpu_load", "mem_used")
   395→//   - statType: Type of statistic: "avg", "min", or "max"
   396→//
   397→// Returns the requested statistic value or 0.0 if not found.
   398→func LoadJobStat(job *schema.Job, metric string, statType string) float64 {
   399→	if stats, ok := job.Statistics[metric]; ok {
   400→		switch statType {
   401→		case "avg":
   402→			return stats.Avg
   403→		case "max":
   404→			return stats.Max
   405→		case "min":
   406→			return stats.Min
   407→		default:
   408→			cclog.Errorf("Unknown stat type %s", statType)
   409→		}
   410→	}
   411→
   412→	return 0.0
   413→}

   415→// JobCountGrouped counts jobs grouped by a dimension without computing detailed statistics.
   416→//
   417→// This is a lightweight alternative to JobsStatsGrouped when only job counts are needed,
   418→// avoiding the overhead of calculating walltime and resource usage metrics.
   419→//
   420→// Parameters:
   421→//   - ctx: Context for security checks
   422→//   - filter: Filters to apply
   423→//   - groupBy: Grouping dimension (User, Project, Cluster, or SubCluster)
   424→//
   425→// Returns JobsStatistics with only ID and TotalJobs populated for each group.
   426→func (r *JobRepository) JobCountGrouped(
   427→	ctx context.Context,
   428→	filter []*model.JobFilter,
   429→	groupBy *model.Aggregate,
   430→) ([]*model.JobsStatistics, error) {
   431→	start := time.Now()
   432→	col := groupBy2column[*groupBy]
   433→	query := r.buildCountQuery(filter, "", col)
   434→	query, err := SecurityCheck(ctx, query)
   435→	if err != nil {
   436→		return nil, err
   437→	}
   438→	rows, err := query.RunWith(r.DB).Query()
   439→	if err != nil {
   440→		cclog.Warn("Error while querying DB for job statistics")
   441→		return nil, err
   442→	}
   443→
   444→	stats := make([]*model.JobsStatistics, 0, 100)
   445→
   446→	for rows.Next() {
   447→		var id sql.NullString
   448→		var cnt sql.NullInt64
   449→		if err := rows.Scan(&id, &cnt); err != nil {
   450→			cclog.Warn("Error while scanning rows")
   451→			return nil, err
   452→		}
   453→		if id.Valid {

   462→	cclog.Debugf("Timer JobCountGrouped %s", time.Since(start))

   465→
   466→// AddJobCountGrouped augments existing statistics with additional job counts by category.
   467→//
   468→// This method enriches JobsStatistics returned by JobsStatsGrouped or JobCountGrouped
   469→// with counts of running or short-running (based on ShortRunningJobsDuration) jobs, matched by group ID.
   470→//
   471→// Parameters:
   472→//   - ctx: Context for security checks
   473→//   - filter: Filters to apply
   474→//   - groupBy: Grouping dimension (must match the dimension used for stats parameter)
   475→//   - stats: Existing statistics to augment (modified in-place by ID matching)
   476→//   - kind: "running" to add RunningJobs count, "short" to add ShortJobs count
   477→//
   478→// Returns the same stats slice with RunningJobs or ShortJobs fields populated per group.
   479→// Groups without matching jobs will have 0 for the added field.
   480→func (r *JobRepository) AddJobCountGrouped(
   481→	ctx context.Context,
   482→	filter []*model.JobFilter,
   483→	groupBy *model.Aggregate,
   484→	stats []*model.JobsStatistics,
   485→	kind string,
   486→) ([]*model.JobsStatistics, error) {
   487→	start := time.Now()
   488→	col := groupBy2column[*groupBy]
   489→	query := r.buildCountQuery(filter, kind, col)
   490→	query, err := SecurityCheck(ctx, query)
   491→	if err != nil {
   492→		return nil, err
   493→	}
   494→	rows, err := query.RunWith(r.DB).Query()
   495→	if err != nil {
   496→		cclog.Warn("Error while querying DB for job statistics")
   497→		return nil, err
   498→	}
   499→
   500→	counts := make(map[string]int)
   501→
   502→	for rows.Next() {
   503→		var id sql.NullString
   504→		var cnt sql.NullInt64
   505→		if err := rows.Scan(&id, &cnt); err != nil {
   506→			cclog.Warn("Error while scanning rows")
   507→			return nil, err
   508→		}
   509→		if id.Valid {
   510→			counts[id.String] = int(cnt.Int64)
   511→		}
   512→	}
   513→
   514→	switch kind {
   515→	case "running":
   516→		for _, s := range stats {
   517→			s.RunningJobs = counts[s.ID]
   518→		}
   519→	case "short":
   520→		for _, s := range stats {
   521→			s.ShortJobs = counts[s.ID]
   522→		}
   523→	}
   524→
   525→	cclog.Debugf("Timer AddJobCountGrouped %s", time.Since(start))
   526→	return stats, nil
   527→}
   528→
   529→// AddJobCount augments existing overall statistics with additional job counts by category.
   530→//
   531→// Similar to AddJobCountGrouped but for ungrouped statistics. Applies the same count
   532→// to all statistics entries (typically just one).
   533→//
   534→// Parameters:
   535→//   - ctx: Context for security checks
   536→//   - filter: Filters to apply
   537→//   - stats: Existing statistics to augment (modified in-place)
   538→//   - kind: "running" to add RunningJobs count, "short" to add ShortJobs count
   539→//
   540→// Returns the same stats slice with RunningJobs or ShortJobs fields set to the total count.
   541→func (r *JobRepository) AddJobCount(
   542→	ctx context.Context,
   543→	filter []*model.JobFilter,
   544→	stats []*model.JobsStatistics,
   545→	kind string,
   546→) ([]*model.JobsStatistics, error) {
   547→	start := time.Now()
   548→	query := r.buildCountQuery(filter, kind, "")
   549→	query, err := SecurityCheck(ctx, query)
   550→	if err != nil {
   551→		return nil, err
   552→	}
   553→	rows, err := query.RunWith(r.DB).Query()
   554→	if err != nil {
   555→		cclog.Warn("Error while querying DB for job statistics")
   556→		return nil, err
   557→	}
   558→
   559→	var count int
   560→
   561→	for rows.Next() {
   562→		var cnt sql.NullInt64
   563→		if err := rows.Scan(&cnt); err != nil {
   564→			cclog.Warn("Error while scanning rows")
   565→			return nil, err
   566→		}
   567→
   568→		count = int(cnt.Int64)
   569→	}
   570→
   571→	switch kind {
   572→	case "running":
   573→		for _, s := range stats {
   574→			s.RunningJobs = count
   575→		}
   576→	case "short":
   577→		for _, s := range stats {
   578→			s.ShortJobs = count
   579→		}
   580→	}
   581→
   582→	cclog.Debugf("Timer AddJobCount %s", time.Since(start))
   583→	return stats, nil
   584→}
   585→
   586→// AddHistograms augments statistics with distribution histograms for job properties.
   587→//
   588→// Generates histogram data for visualization of job duration, node count, core count,
   589→// and accelerator count distributions. Duration histogram uses intelligent binning based
   590→// on the requested resolution.
   591→//
   592→// Parameters:
   593→//   - ctx: Context for security checks
   594→//   - filter: Filters to apply to jobs included in histograms
   595→//   - stat: Statistics struct to augment (modified in-place)
   596→//   - durationBins: Bin size - "1m", "10m", "1h", "6h", "12h", or "24h" (default)
   597→//
   598→// Populates these fields in stat:
   599→//   - HistDuration: Job duration distribution (zero-padded bins)
   600→//   - HistNumNodes: Node count distribution
   601→//   - HistNumCores: Core (hwthread) count distribution
   602→//   - HistNumAccs: Accelerator count distribution
   603→//
   604→// Duration bins are pre-initialized with zeros to ensure consistent ranges for visualization.
   605→// Bin size determines both the width and maximum duration displayed (e.g., "1h" = 48 bins × 1h = 48h max).
   606→func (r *JobRepository) AddHistograms(
   607→	ctx context.Context,
   608→	filter []*model.JobFilter,
   609→	stat *model.JobsStatistics,
   610→	durationBins *string,
   611→) (*model.JobsStatistics, error) {
   612→	start := time.Now()
   613→
   614→	var targetBinCount int
   615→	var targetBinSize int
   616→	switch *durationBins {
   617→	case "1m": // 1 Minute Bins + Max 60 Bins -> Max 60 Minutes
   618→		targetBinCount = 60
   619→		targetBinSize = 60
   620→	case "10m": // 10 Minute Bins + Max 72 Bins -> Max 12 Hours
   621→		targetBinCount = 72
   622→		targetBinSize = 600
   623→	case "1h": // 1 Hour Bins + Max 48 Bins -> Max 48 Hours
   624→		targetBinCount = 48
   625→		targetBinSize = 3600
   626→	case "6h": // 6 Hour Bins + Max 12 Bins -> Max 3 Days
   627→		targetBinCount = 12
   628→		targetBinSize = 21600
   629→	case "12h": // 12 hour Bins + Max 14 Bins -> Max 7 Days
   630→		targetBinCount = 14
   631→		targetBinSize = 43200
   632→	default: // 24h
   633→		targetBinCount = 24
   634→		targetBinSize = 3600
   635→	}
   636→
   637→	var err error
   638→	// Return X-Values always as seconds, will be formatted into minutes and hours in frontend
   639→	value := fmt.Sprintf(`CAST(ROUND(((CASE WHEN job.job_state = "running" THEN %d - job.start_time ELSE job.duration END) / %d) + 1) as int) as value`, time.Now().Unix(), targetBinSize)
   640→	stat.HistDuration, err = r.jobsDurationStatisticsHistogram(ctx, value, filter, targetBinSize, &targetBinCount)
   641→	if err != nil {
   642→		cclog.Warn("Error while loading job statistics histogram: job duration")
   643→		return nil, err
   644→	}
   645→
   646→	stat.HistNumNodes, err = r.jobsStatisticsHistogram(ctx, "job.num_nodes as value", filter)
   647→	if err != nil {
   648→		cclog.Warn("Error while loading job statistics histogram: num nodes")
   649→		return nil, err
   650→	}
   651→
   652→	stat.HistNumCores, err = r.jobsStatisticsHistogram(ctx, "job.num_hwthreads as value", filter)
   653→	if err != nil {
   654→		cclog.Warn("Error while loading job statistics histogram: num hwthreads")
   655→		return nil, err
   656→	}
   657→
   658→	stat.HistNumAccs, err = r.jobsStatisticsHistogram(ctx, "job.num_acc as value", filter)
   659→	if err != nil {
   660→		cclog.Warn("Error while loading job statistics histogram: num acc")
   661→		return nil, err
   662→	}
   663→
   664→	cclog.Debugf("Timer AddHistograms %s", time.Since(start))
   665→	return stat, nil
   666→}
   667→
   668→// AddMetricHistograms augments statistics with distribution histograms for job metrics.
   669→//
   670→// Generates histogram data for metrics like CPU load, memory usage, etc. Handles running
   671→// and completed jobs differently: running jobs load data from metric backend, completed jobs
   672→// use footprint data from database.
   673→//
   674→// Parameters:
   675→//   - ctx: Context for security checks
   676→//   - filter: Filters to apply (MUST contain State filter for running jobs)
   677→//   - metrics: List of metric names to histogram (e.g., ["cpu_load", "mem_used"])
   678→//   - stat: Statistics struct to augment (modified in-place)
   679→//   - targetBinCount: Number of histogram bins (default: 10)
   680→//
   681→// Populates HistMetrics field in stat with MetricHistoPoints for each metric.
   682→//
   683→// Binning algorithm:
   684→//   - Values normalized to metric's peak value from cluster configuration
   685→//   - Bins evenly distributed from 0 to peak
   686→//   - Pre-initialized with zeros for consistent visualization
   687→//
   688→// Limitations:
   689→//   - Running jobs: Limited to 5000 jobs for performance
   690→//   - Requires valid cluster configuration with metric peak values
   691→//   - Uses footprint statistic (avg/max/min) configured per metric
   692→func (r *JobRepository) AddMetricHistograms(
   693→	ctx context.Context,
   694→	filter []*model.JobFilter,
   695→	metrics []string,
   696→	stat *model.JobsStatistics,
   697→	targetBinCount *int,
   698→) (*model.JobsStatistics, error) {
   699→	start := time.Now()
   700→
   701→	// Running Jobs Only: First query jobdata from sqlite, then query data and make bins
   702→	for _, f := range filter {
   703→		if f.State != nil {
   704→			if len(f.State) == 1 && f.State[0] == "running" {
   705→				stat.HistMetrics = r.runningJobsMetricStatisticsHistogram(ctx, metrics, filter, targetBinCount)
   706→				cclog.Debugf("Timer AddMetricHistograms %s", time.Since(start))
   707→				return stat, nil
   708→			}
   709→		}
   710→	}
   711→
   712→	// All other cases: Query and make bins in sqlite directly
   713→	for _, m := range metrics {
   714→		metricHisto, err := r.jobsMetricStatisticsHistogram(ctx, m, filter, targetBinCount)
   715→		if err != nil {
   716→			cclog.Warnf("Error while loading job metric statistics histogram: %s", m)
   717→			continue
   718→		}
   719→		stat.HistMetrics = append(stat.HistMetrics, metricHisto)
   720→	}
   721→
   722→	cclog.Debugf("Timer AddMetricHistograms %s", time.Since(start))
   723→	return stat, nil
   724→}
   725→
   726→// jobsStatisticsHistogram generates a simple histogram by grouping on a column value.
   727→//
   728→// Used for histograms where the column value directly represents the bin (e.g., node count, core count).
   729→// Unlike duration/metric histograms, this doesn't pre-initialize bins with zeros.
   730→//
   731→// Parameters:
   732→//   - value: SQL expression that produces the histogram value, aliased as "value"
   733→//   - filters: Job filters to apply
   734→//
   735→// Returns histogram points with Value (from column) and Count (number of jobs).
   736→func (r *JobRepository) jobsStatisticsHistogram(
   737→	ctx context.Context,
   738→	value string,
   739→	filters []*model.JobFilter,
   740→) ([]*model.HistoPoint, error) {
   741→	start := time.Now()
   742→	query, qerr := SecurityCheck(ctx,
   743→		sq.Select(value, "COUNT(job.id) AS count").From("job"))
   744→
   745→	if qerr != nil {
   746→		return nil, qerr
   747→	}
   748→
   749→	for _, f := range filters {
   750→		query = BuildWhereClause(f, query)
   751→	}
   752→
   753→	rows, err := query.GroupBy("value").RunWith(r.DB).Query()
   754→	if err != nil {
   755→		cclog.Error("Error while running query")
   756→		return nil, err
   757→	}
   758→
   759→	points := make([]*model.HistoPoint, 0)
   760→	// is it possible to introduce zero values here? requires info about bincount
   761→	for rows.Next() {
   762→		point := model.HistoPoint{}
   763→		if err := rows.Scan(&point.Value, &point.Count); err != nil {
   764→			cclog.Warn("Error while scanning rows")
   765→			return nil, err
   766→		}
   767→
   768→		points = append(points, &point)
   769→	}
   770→	cclog.Debugf("Timer jobsStatisticsHistogram %s", time.Since(start))
   771→	return points, nil
   772→}
   773→
   774→// jobsDurationStatisticsHistogram generates a duration histogram with pre-initialized bins.
   775→//
   776→// Bins are zero-padded to provide consistent ranges for visualization, unlike simple
   777→// histograms which only return bins with data. The value parameter should compute
   778→// the bin number from job duration.
   779→//
   780→// Parameters:
   781→//   - value: SQL expression computing bin number from duration, aliased as "value"
   782→//   - filters: Job filters to apply
   783→//   - binSizeSeconds: Width of each bin in seconds
   784→//   - targetBinCount: Number of bins to pre-initialize
   785→//
   786→// Returns histogram points with Value (bin_number × binSizeSeconds) and Count.
   787→// All bins from 1 to targetBinCount are returned, with Count=0 for empty bins.
   788→//
   789→// Algorithm:
   790→//  1. Pre-initialize targetBinCount bins with zero counts
   791→//  2. Query database for actual counts per bin
   792→//  3. Match query results to pre-initialized bins by value
   793→//  4. Bins without matches remain at zero
   794→func (r *JobRepository) jobsDurationStatisticsHistogram(
   795→	ctx context.Context,
   796→	value string,
   797→	filters []*model.JobFilter,
   798→	binSizeSeconds int,
   799→	targetBinCount *int,
   800→) ([]*model.HistoPoint, error) {
   801→	start := time.Now()
   802→	query, qerr := SecurityCheck(ctx,
   803→		sq.Select(value, "COUNT(job.id) AS count").From("job"))
   804→
   805→	if qerr != nil {
   806→		return nil, qerr
   807→	}
   808→
   809→	// Each bin represents a duration range: bin N = [N*binSizeSeconds, (N+1)*binSizeSeconds)
   810→	// Example: binSizeSeconds=3600 (1 hour), bin 1 = 0-1h, bin 2 = 1-2h, etc.
   811→	points := make([]*model.HistoPoint, 0)
   812→	for i := 1; i <= *targetBinCount; i++ {
   813→		point := model.HistoPoint{Value: i * binSizeSeconds, Count: 0}
   814→		points = append(points, &point)
   815→	}
   816→
   817→	for _, f := range filters {
   818→		query = BuildWhereClause(f, query)
   819→	}
   820→
   821→	rows, err := query.GroupBy("value").RunWith(r.DB).Query()
   822→	if err != nil {
   823→		cclog.Error("Error while running query")
   824→		return nil, err
   825→	}
   826→
   827→	// Match query results to pre-initialized bins.
   828→	// point.Value from query is the bin number; multiply by binSizeSeconds to match bin.Value.
   829→	for rows.Next() {
   830→		point := model.HistoPoint{}
   831→		if err := rows.Scan(&point.Value, &point.Count); err != nil {
   832→			cclog.Warn("Error while scanning rows")
   833→			return nil, err
   834→		}
   835→
   836→		for _, e := range points {
   837→			if e.Value == (point.Value * binSizeSeconds) {
   838→				e.Count = point.Count
   839→				break
   840→			}
   841→		}
   842→	}
   843→
   844→	cclog.Debugf("Timer jobsStatisticsHistogram %s", time.Since(start))
   845→	return points, nil
   846→}
   847→
   848→// jobsMetricStatisticsHistogram generates a metric histogram using footprint data from completed jobs.
   849→//
   850→// Values are normalized to the metric's peak value and distributed into bins. The algorithm
   851→// is based on SQL histogram generation techniques, extracting metric values from JSON footprint
   852→// and computing bin assignments in SQL.
   853→//
   854→// Parameters:
   855→//   - metric: Metric name (e.g., "cpu_load", "mem_used")
   856→//   - filters: Job filters to apply
   857→//   - bins: Number of bins to generate
   858→//
   859→// Returns MetricHistoPoints with metric name, unit, footprint stat type, and binned data.
   860→//
   861→// Algorithm:
   862→//  1. Determine peak value from cluster configuration (filtered cluster or max across all)
   863→//  2. Generate SQL that extracts footprint value, normalizes to [0,1], multiplies by bin count
   864→//  3. Pre-initialize bins with min/max ranges based on peak value
   865→//  4. Query database for counts per bin
   866→//  5. Match results to pre-initialized bins
   867→//
   868→// Special handling: Values exactly equal to peak are forced into the last bin by multiplying
   869→// peak by 0.999999999 to avoid creating an extra bin.
   870→func (r *JobRepository) jobsMetricStatisticsHistogram(
   871→	ctx context.Context,
   872→	metric string,
   873→	filters []*model.JobFilter,
   874→	bins *int,
   875→) (*model.MetricHistoPoints, error) {
   876→	// Peak value defines the upper bound for binning: values are distributed across
   877→	// bins from 0 to peak. First try to get peak from filtered cluster, otherwise
   878→	// scan all clusters to find the maximum peak value.
   879→	var metricConfig *schema.MetricConfig
   880→	var peak float64
   881→	var unit string
   882→	var footprintStat string
   883→
   884→	// Try to get metric config from filtered cluster
   885→	for _, f := range filters {
   886→		if f.Cluster != nil {
   887→			metricConfig = archive.GetMetricConfig(*f.Cluster.Eq, metric)
   888→			peak = metricConfig.Peak
   889→			unit = metricConfig.Unit.Prefix + metricConfig.Unit.Base
   890→			footprintStat = metricConfig.Footprint
   891→			cclog.Debugf("Cluster %s filter found with peak %f for %s", *f.Cluster.Eq, peak, metric)
   892→		}
   893→	}
   894→
   895→	// If no cluster filter or peak not found, find largest peak across all clusters
   896→	// This ensures histogram can accommodate all possible values
   897→	if peak == 0.0 {
   898→		for _, c := range archive.Clusters {
   899→			for _, m := range c.MetricConfig {
   900→				if m.Name == metric {
   901→					if m.Peak > peak {
   902→						peak = m.Peak
   903→					}
   904→					if unit == "" {
   905→						unit = m.Unit.Prefix + m.Unit.Base
   906→					}
   907→					if footprintStat == "" {
   908→						footprintStat = m.Footprint
   909→					}
   910→				}
   911→			}
   912→		}
   913→	}
   914→
   915→	// Construct SQL histogram bins using normalized values.
   916→	// Algorithm based on: https://jereze.com/code/sql-histogram/ (modified)
   917→	start := time.Now()
   918→
   919→	// Bin calculation formula:
   920→	//   bin_number = CAST( (value / peak) * num_bins AS INTEGER ) + 1
   921→	// Special case: value == peak would create bin N+1, so we test for equality
   922→	// and multiply peak by 0.999999999 to force it into bin N.
   923→	binQuery := fmt.Sprintf(`CAST(
   924→		((case when json_extract(footprint, "$.%s") = %f then %f*0.999999999 else json_extract(footprint, "$.%s") end) / %f)
   925→		* %v as INTEGER )`,
   926→		(metric + "_" + footprintStat), peak, peak, (metric + "_" + footprintStat), peak, *bins)
   927→
   928→	mainQuery := sq.Select(
   929→		fmt.Sprintf(`%s + 1 as bin`, binQuery),
   930→		`count(*) as count`,
   931→	).From("job").Where(
   932→		"JSON_VALID(footprint)",
   933→	).Where(fmt.Sprintf(`json_extract(footprint, "$.%s") is not null and json_extract(footprint, "$.%s") <= %f`, (metric + "_" + footprintStat), (metric + "_" + footprintStat), peak))
   934→
   935→	mainQuery, qerr := SecurityCheck(ctx, mainQuery)
   936→	if qerr != nil {
   937→		return nil, qerr
   938→	}
   939→
   940→	for _, f := range filters {
   941→		mainQuery = BuildWhereClause(f, mainQuery)
   942→	}
   943→
   944→	mainQuery = mainQuery.GroupBy("bin").OrderBy("bin")
   945→
   946→	rows, err := mainQuery.RunWith(r.DB).Query()
   947→	if err != nil {
   948→		cclog.Errorf("Error while running mainQuery: %s", err)
   949→		return nil, err
   950→	}
   951→
   952→	// Pre-initialize bins with calculated min/max ranges.
   953→	// Example: peak=1000, bins=10 -> bin 1=[0,100), bin 2=[100,200), ..., bin 10=[900,1000]
   954→	points := make([]*model.MetricHistoPoint, 0)
   955→	binStep := int(peak) / *bins
   956→	for i := 1; i <= *bins; i++ {
   957→		binMin := (binStep * (i - 1))
   958→		binMax := (binStep * i)
   959→		epoint := model.MetricHistoPoint{Bin: &i, Count: 0, Min: &binMin, Max: &binMax}
   960→		points = append(points, &epoint)
   961→	}
   962→
   963→	// Match query results to pre-initialized bins.
   964→	for rows.Next() {
   965→		rpoint := model.MetricHistoPoint{}
   966→		if err := rows.Scan(&rpoint.Bin, &rpoint.Count); err != nil {
   967→			cclog.Warnf("Error while scanning rows for %s", metric)
   968→			return nil, err
   969→		}
   970→
   971→		for _, e := range points {
   972→			if e.Bin != nil && rpoint.Bin != nil && *e.Bin == *rpoint.Bin {
   973→				e.Count = rpoint.Count
   974→				break
   975→			}
   976→		}
   977→	}
   978→
   979→	result := model.MetricHistoPoints{Metric: metric, Unit: unit, Stat: &footprintStat, Data: points}
   980→
   981→	cclog.Debugf("Timer jobsStatisticsHistogram %s", time.Since(start))
   982→	return &result, nil
   983→}
   984→
   985→// runningJobsMetricStatisticsHistogram generates metric histograms for running jobs using live data.
   986→//
   987→// Unlike completed jobs which use footprint data from the database, running jobs require
   988→// fetching current metric averages from the metric backend (via metricdispatch).
   989→//
   990→// Parameters:
   991→//   - metrics: List of metric names
   992→//   - filters: Job filters (should filter to running jobs only)
   993→//   - bins: Number of histogram bins
   994→//
   995→// Returns slice of MetricHistoPoints, one per metric.
   996→//
   997→// Limitations:
   998→//   - Maximum 5000 jobs (returns nil if more jobs match)
   999→//   - Requires metric backend availability
  1000→//   - Bins based on metric peak values from cluster configuration
  1001→//
  1002→// Algorithm:
  1003→//  1. Query first 5001 jobs to check count limit
  1004→//  2. Load metric averages for all jobs via metricdispatch
  1005→//  3. For each metric, create bins based on peak value
  1006→//  4. Iterate averages and count jobs per bin
  1007→func (r *JobRepository) runningJobsMetricStatisticsHistogram(
  1008→	ctx context.Context,
  1009→	metrics []string,
  1010→	filters []*model.JobFilter,
  1011→	bins *int,
  1012→) []*model.MetricHistoPoints {
  1013→	// Get Jobs
  1014→	jobs, err := r.QueryJobs(ctx, filters, &model.PageRequest{Page: 1, ItemsPerPage: 5000 + 1}, nil)
  1015→	if err != nil {
  1016→		cclog.Errorf("Error while querying jobs for footprint: %s", err)
  1017→		return nil
  1018→	}
  1019→	if len(jobs) > 5000 {
  1020→		cclog.Errorf("too many jobs matched (max: %d)", 5000)
  1021→		return nil
  1022→	}
  1023→
  1024→	// Get AVGs from metric repo
  1025→	avgs := make([][]schema.Float, len(metrics))
  1026→	for i := range avgs {
  1027→		avgs[i] = make([]schema.Float, 0, len(jobs))
  1028→	}
  1029→
  1030→	for _, job := range jobs {
  1031→		if job.MonitoringStatus == schema.MonitoringStatusDisabled || job.MonitoringStatus == schema.MonitoringStatusArchivingFailed {
  1032→			continue
  1033→		}
  1034→
  1035→		if err := metricdispatch.LoadAverages(job, metrics, avgs, ctx); err != nil {
  1036→			cclog.Errorf("Error while loading averages for histogram: %s", err)
  1037→			return nil
  1038→		}
  1039→	}
  1040→
  1041→	// Iterate metrics to fill endresult
  1042→	data := make([]*model.MetricHistoPoints, 0)
  1043→	for idx, metric := range metrics {
  1044→		// Get specific Peak or largest Peak
  1045→		var metricConfig *schema.MetricConfig
  1046→		var peak float64
  1047→		var unit string
  1048→
  1049→		for _, f := range filters {
  1050→			if f.Cluster != nil {
  1051→				metricConfig = archive.GetMetricConfig(*f.Cluster.Eq, metric)
  1052→				peak = metricConfig.Peak
  1053→				unit = metricConfig.Unit.Prefix + metricConfig.Unit.Base
  1054→			}
  1055→		}
  1056→
  1057→		if peak == 0.0 {
  1058→			for _, c := range archive.Clusters {
  1059→				for _, m := range c.MetricConfig {
  1060→					if m.Name == metric {
  1061→						if m.Peak > peak {
  1062→							peak = m.Peak
  1063→						}
  1064→						if unit == "" {
  1065→							unit = m.Unit.Prefix + m.Unit.Base
  1066→						}
  1067→					}
  1068→				}
  1069→			}
  1070→		}
  1071→
  1072→		// Make and fill bins
  1073→		peakBin := int(peak) / *bins
  1074→
  1075→		points := make([]*model.MetricHistoPoint, 0)
  1076→		for b := 0; b < *bins; b++ {
  1077→			count := 0
  1078→			bindex := b + 1
  1079→			bmin := peakBin * b
  1080→			bmax := peakBin * (b + 1)
  1081→
  1082→			// Iterate AVG values for indexed metric and count for bins
  1083→			for _, val := range avgs[idx] {
  1084→				if int(val) >= bmin && int(val) < bmax {
  1085→					count += 1
  1086→				}
  1087→			}
  1088→
  1089→			// Append Bin to Metric Result Array
  1090→			point := model.MetricHistoPoint{Bin: &bindex, Count: count, Min: &bmin, Max: &bmax}
  1091→			points = append(points, &point)
  1092→		}
  1093→
  1094→		// Append Metric Result Array to final results array
  1095→		result := model.MetricHistoPoints{Metric: metric, Unit: unit, Data: points}
  1096→		data = append(data, &result)
  1097→	}
  1098→
  1099→	return data
  1100→}
